units.name: Remove 3 coding-ability tasks
units.description: Delete the 3 task directories that test coding ability instead of context retrieval: beam-pipeline-builder-refac-001, ansible-abc-imports-fix-001, aspnetcore-code-review-001. Also remove any references to these tasks in config files.
acceptance: Directory benchmarks/technical_debt/beam-pipeline-builder-refac-001/ does not exist
Directory benchmarks/incident_response/ansible-abc-imports-fix-001/ does not exist
Directory benchmarks/feature_delivery/aspnetcore-code-review-001/ does not exist
grep -r 'beam-pipeline-builder-refac-001\|ansible-abc-imports-fix-001\|aspnetcore-code-review-001' benchmarks/ configs/ returns no matches
All remaining task.toml files still pass: python scripts/validate_tasks_preflight.py (or at minimum parse without error)

units.name: Distinguish infra failures from agent failures
units.description: In scripts/orchestration/run_task.py, classify OOM (exit 137), disk full, and timeout (exit 124) as infra_error status instead of score=0.00 completed. Currently agent_exit != 0 sets failure_class='agent_error' for ALL non-zero exits. Add specific handling: exit 137 → infra_oom, exit 124 → infra_timeout (already partially handled). Ensure these produce infra_ prefixed failure_class and result.success=False with phase='infra_error', not 'complete'.
acceptance: When agent exits with code 137, result has failure_class='infra_oom' and phase != 'complete'
When agent exits with code 124, result has failure_class='infra_timeout' and phase != 'complete'
When agent exits with code 1 (normal failure), result has failure_class='agent_error' as before
Tests exist in a test file that verify exit code 137/124 produce correct failure_class values
Existing tests still pass

units.name: Create pinned SHA manifest with staleness detection
units.description: Create configs/repo_versions.json with SHA + last-verified date for all repos referenced by tasks. Build a script scripts/infra/check_repo_staleness.py that reads repo_versions.json and flags repos >6 months behind HEAD. The manifest should be generated by scanning all task.toml files for repo URLs and revs.
acceptance: configs/repo_versions.json exists with SHA and last_verified date for all repos found in benchmarks/*/task.toml
Each entry in repo_versions.json has fields: url, pinned_rev, last_verified (ISO date string)
scripts/infra/check_repo_staleness.py exists and can be run with: python scripts/infra/check_repo_staleness.py
Running check_repo_staleness.py with a test manifest where last_verified is >6 months ago outputs a warning for that repo
Tests exist for the staleness check logic

units.name: Add --mode argument to chain_runner.py
units.description: The chain_runner.py currently only accepts --simulate but not --mode (baseline/mcp_only/hybrid). Add --mode argument support that passes through to the underlying session runner, mirroring how run_task.py handles modes.
acceptance: python scripts/orchestration/chain_runner.py --help shows --mode argument with choices baseline/mcp_only/hybrid
python scripts/orchestration/chain_runner.py --mode hybrid --help does not error
The mode value is passed through to SessionConfig and used in run_session calls
Tests exist verifying --mode argument parsing

units.name: Fix Sourcegraph indexing for all repos
units.description: Update configs/sg_indexing_list.json to mark repos as indexed. The current state shows _indexed_count: 0 across all suites. Write a script scripts/infra/verify_sg_indexing.py that checks each repo in the indexing list against the Sourcegraph API (or marks them for verification). Update the _indexed_count fields. The acceptance criterion from PRD: jq '[.[] | select(._indexed == true)] | length' should return 131+. Since we can't actually call the SG API here, create the verification script and update the indexing list structure to support per-repo _indexed flags.
acceptance: configs/sg_indexing_list.json has per-repo entries with _indexed boolean field
scripts/infra/verify_sg_indexing.py exists and reads sg_indexing_list.json
verify_sg_indexing.py outputs a summary of indexed vs pending repos
The JSON structure supports tracking individual repo indexing status
Tests exist for the verification script logic

units.name: Implement Cross-Repo Necessity Test framework
units.description: Create a CRNT validation tool that tests whether multi-repo tasks actually require multiple repos. For each multi-repo task, it should support ablation testing: remove one repo and verify agent score drops to ≤60%. Create scripts/validation/crnt_validator.py with the CRNT logic. It should: (1) read a task.toml, (2) identify repos, (3) generate ablation configs (task.toml variants with one repo removed), (4) define the pass criteria (score ≤60% when ablated). Also create a CRNT checklist schema addition.
acceptance: scripts/validation/crnt_validator.py exists
crnt_validator.py can parse a task.toml and list its repos
crnt_validator.py can generate ablation task configs (one per repo removed)
crnt_validator.py defines CRNT pass criteria: ablated score ≤60%
Running python scripts/validation/crnt_validator.py --help shows usage
Tests exist for the CRNT logic (parsing, ablation config generation, pass criteria evaluation)

units.name: Author 10 multi-repo task definitions for 5 zero-coverage types
units.description: Create task.toml definitions for multi-repo variants of the 5 task types that currently have zero multi-repo representation: config_drift, db_schema_evolution, dead_code_necropsy, error_provenance, support_code_mapping. Minimum 2 per type = 10 tasks. Each task must use real OSS repos with actual dependency chains, specify difficulty_stratum as dual_repo or tri_repo, include proper checkpoints, and follow the task schema. Use diverse ecosystems (not just K8s/gRPC). Tasks should use the 'investigate' pattern where applicable.
acceptance: At least 2 new task.toml files exist for each of: config_drift, db_schema_evolution, dead_code_necropsy, error_provenance, support_code_mapping
All new task.toml files have difficulty_stratum set to dual_repo or tri_repo
All new tasks reference real GitHub repos (verifiable URLs)
All new tasks have at least 2 checkpoints with ground_truth
find benchmarks/ -name task.toml | xargs grep -l 'stratum.*dual_repo\|stratum.*tri_repo' returns files for all 5 types
At least 4 of 10 new tasks use the 'investigate' multi_repo_pattern
New tasks span at least 3 distinct ecosystem chains (not all K8s/gRPC)
All new task.toml files validate against schemas/task.schema.json
Each new task has a test.sh verification script
